posts.name: Alexis Kalogeropoulos
posts.text:
Dear experts
Apologies if this has been answered already, but I would like to ask if there are some links/examples on how to properly perform a signal injection test for different \mu values, as I could not find anything searching the usual git combine documentation.
Thanks in advance,
Alexis
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts

Apologies if this has been answered already, but I would like to ask if there are some links/examples on how to properly perform a signal injection test for different \mu values, as I could not find anything searching the usual git combine documentation.

Thanks in advance,
Alexis

posts.name: Andrea Carlo Marini
posts.text:
Hi Alexis,
--expectSignal 2 or --setParameters r=2,...  with either the toys or Asimov generation.
cheers,
-Andrea
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Alexis,

--expectSignal 2 or --setParameters r=2,... with either the toys or Asimov generation.

cheers,
-Andrea

posts.name: Alexis Kalogeropoulos
posts.text:
so, for a set of different signal strengths like SIGNAL_STRENGTHS=(0.0 0.5 1.0 1.5 2.0) would this be ok?
combineTool.py -M FitDiagnostics \
-d input_Run2_3000_SRCR1B2B.root \
-t $TOYS_PER_JOB \
--toysFrequentist \
--expectSignal=$EXPECT_SIG \
--setParameters r=$EXPECT_SIG \
--cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 1 \
-s $JOB_SEED \
--rMin -5 --rMax 5 \
-n _sigInj_Suu3_r${NAME}_${BASE_SEED}_part${i} \
-v 1 \
--job-mode condor \
--sub-opts='+JobFlavour="workday"' \
--task-name SignalInjection_${NAME}_part${i}
posts.cooked_html:
so, for a set of different signal strengths like SIGNAL_STRENGTHS=(0.0 0.5 1.0 1.5 2.0) would this be ok?

combineTool.py -M FitDiagnostics \
    -d input_Run2_3000_SRCR1B2B.root \
    -t $TOYS_PER_JOB \
    --toysFrequentist \
    --expectSignal=$EXPECT_SIG \
    --setParameters r=$EXPECT_SIG \
    --cminDefaultMinimizerStrategy 1 \
    -s $JOB_SEED \
    --rMin -5 --rMax 5 \
    -n _sigInj_Suu3_r${NAME}_${BASE_SEED}_part${i} \
    -v 1 \
    --job-mode condor \
    --sub-opts='+JobFlavour="workday"' \
    --task-name SignalInjection_${NAME}_part${i}
